trade.summary: Arabia Saudita is Giappone's largest single source of crude oil, accounting for roughly 40% of Giappone's oil imports. This energy dependency fundamentally shapes the bilateral relationship. Giappone exports automobiles (Toyota, Nissan, Honda dominate Saudi market), machinery, and electronics to Arabia Saudita. Trade is highly asymmetric, dominated by Saudi hydrocarbon exports.

diplomatic.summary: Giappone-Saudi relations are primarily driven by energy security imperatives. Giappone maintains careful neutrality on Middle Eastern political disputes to preserve hydrocarbon supply. Arabia Saudita is interested in Giapponeese technology for Vision 2030 diversification. High-level exchanges are regular. Giappone has largely abstained from criticizing Saudi human rights practices to protect energy ties.

military.summary: No formal military alliance. Giappone maintains strict policies against arms exports, though these have been relaxed in recent years. No significant arms transfers to Arabia Saudita. Giappone's constitutional limitations on overseas military deployments prevent meaningful defense cooperation. Giappone does coordinate on maritime security in the Persian Gulf region to protect its oil tanker routes.

financial.summary: Arabia Saudita's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has made significant investments in Giapponeese technology and entertainment companies. Saudi Aramco has strategic partnerships with Giapponeese refiners (ENEOS, Idemitsu). Giapponeese trading companies (Mitsui, Mitsubishi, JXTG) have joint ventures in Saudi petrochemical projects. Vision 2030 has attracted Giapponeese infrastructure investment interest.

energy.summary: Arabia Saudita is the cornerstone of Giappone's energy security. Saudi Aramco supplies approximately 38-42% of Giappone's crude oil imports under long-term contracts. The two countries also cooperate on hydrogen energy, with Arabia Saudita positioning itself as a future hydrogen exporter to Giappone. This energy dependency is Giappone's most critical bilateral energy relationship.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation aligned with Saudi Vision 2030's science and technology ambitions. Joint research in renewable energy, desalination, and petrochemical engineering. Giapponeese universities have partnerships with Saudi institutions including KAStati UnitiT. Academic exchanges are expanding.
